results.name: κ-identity across backends — OpfsKappaStore κ ≡ MemKappaStore κ for the same bytes
dedup — the same bytes always yield the same κ (stored once)
distinct — different bytes yield a different κ
contains — present κ true, absent κ false
differential TCK — κ identical AND bytes equal vs the MemKappaStore oracle
pin survives hot-cache pressure (200 puts past HOT_MAX) — pinned κ still resolves
evict is correctness-free — unpinned κ evicts (get→null), pinned κ refuses eviction
PERSISTENCE — after a page reload (in-memory cache gone), the store serves the bytes from OPFS, byte-identical (L3: the store is the memory)

spec: W2 — the browser OpfsKappaStore (os/usr/lib/holo/holo-opfs-kappastore.mjs) implements the holospaces KappaStore contract (put/get/contains/pin) over OPFS, content-addressed by κ. Witnessed in real Chromium: round-trip, κ-identity/dedup, differential vs MemKappaStore, contains/pin/evict, and PERSISTENCE across a page reload (served from OPFS, not memory) — proving Law L3 (the store is the memory) survives a restart in a browser tab.
